titleStmt.title: Letter from Augustine Birrell to Edward W. Mooney, 5 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Augustine Birrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the chief secretary of Ireland, to Edward W. Mooney (b. 1846), an Irish Catholic priest. The letter acknowledges and thanks Mooney for a previous letter he sent recommending Charles P. O'Neill (b.1867) an Irish solicitor and member of the Irish Parliamentary Party for the position of chief crown solicitor due to the death of former solicitor Malchy Kelly (1850-1916).

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5729/10

correspAction.placeName: St. John's Priory, St. John's Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5th April, 1916. Reverend and Dear Sir, Thank you for your letter of yesterday's
date written on behalf of Mr. Charles P. O'Neill, an
applicant for appointment to position of Chief
Crown Solicitor. I am much obliged to you for
the trouble you have taken. Yours faithfully,
Rev. Edward W. Mooney, St. John's Priory,
St. John Street, W.
